query: How frequently in 2026 has Russia launched overnight drone barrages against Ukraine exceeding 600 drones in a single night? List specific dates and drone counts for the largest barrages in May, June, and early July 2026 according to Ukrainian Air Force figures.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the key takeaway:

**Russian overnight drone barrages exceeding 600 drones in 2026** were concentrated in **May and June 2026** — roughly 6–7 identifiable nights, per Ukrainian Air Force figures (compiled by ISIS and reported by CNN/AP/Euronews):

**May 2026** (record month, ~8,161 UAVs total):
- **May 1:** ~619 UAVs
- **May 13:** ~892 UAVs (single-night record)
- **May 13–14:** ~1,560–1,567 drones + 56 missiles cumulative (Zelensky's "largest aerial attack" of the war; 25–27 killed in Kyiv)
- **May 14:** ~675 UAVs
- **May 24:** ~600 UAVs

**June 2026** (~5,749 UAVs total, down ~30%):
- **June 2:** ~656 UAVs
- **June 15:** ~611 UAVs

**Early July 2026** — deadly but **below 600 drones**:
- **July 1–2:** 496 drones (570 total assets)
- **July 3–4:** ~30–31 killed (deadliest capital attack of 2026, but by targeting, not drone count)
- **July 4–6:** 351 drones + 68 missiles

**Two critical caveats I flagged:** (1) The widely cited **741/728-drone record is from July 9, 2025**, not 2026 — don't conflate them. (2) Several large June 2026 figures (660 drones, 1,400+) actually refer to **Ukrainian** attacks *on Russia*, not Russian attacks on Ukraine.

sources.title: Monthly Analysis of Russian Shahed-136 Deployment Against Ukraine (June 2026)
sources.snippet: Compiles Ukrainian Air Force reporting; May over-600 nights (May 1: 619, May 13: 892, May 14: 675, May 24: 600) and June nights (June 2: 656, June 15: 611); May total ~8,161, June ~5,749.

sources.title: 14 May 2026 Russian strikes on Ukraine

sources.title: Russia hammers Ukraine in biggest prolonged drone attack since war began
sources.snippet: Zelensky: Russia fired more than 1,560 drones since the start of Wednesday plus 56 missiles overnight — largest aerial attack in a two-day period.

sources.title: Russia launched record 8,150 Shahed drones at Ukraine in May, analysts say
sources.snippet: OSINT project Oko Hora: at least 8,150 Shahed-type drones used against Ukraine in May 2026; ~7,500 shot down; ~8% reached targets.

sources.title: Ukraine will 'definitely' retaliate for massive Russian attack on Kyiv, Zelenskyy says
sources.snippet: Ukrainian Air Force: Russia launched 570 air attack assets overnight July 1–2, including 496 drones, 24 Iskander ballistic and 4 Zircon missiles.

sources.title: Russia's missile and drone attacks on Ukraine kill at least 22
sources.snippet: Ukraine's air force said Russia fired 351 drones and 68 missiles overnight (July 6); all 29 ballistic missiles struck targets.

sources.title: Russia's latest attack on Kyiv was exceptionally deadly – here is why
sources.snippet: July 3–4 Kyiv attack killed at least 30 — third-deadliest assault on the capital; lethality driven by targeting residential buildings and ballistic missiles.

sources.title: Russia Sets New Record With 1,400+ Drones in Latest Ukraine Barrage
sources.snippet: Russia launched nearly 1,500 drones and missiles in a 24-hour period around May 14, 2026, setting a wartime record.

sources.title: Russia Launches One of Largest Air Assaults Using New Shahed Drone Tactics
sources.snippet: Russia launched at least 800 drones in one of its largest combined assaults (mid-May 2026) using new Shahed tactics.

key_facts: May 2026 was the record month for Russian Shahed-type drone deployment against Ukraine, with roughly 8,150–8,161 UAVs launched over the month (ISIS / OSINT Oko Hora).

The night of 13–14 May 2026 was described by Zelensky as Russia's largest aerial attack since the invasion: ~1,560–1,567 drones plus 56 missiles fired within ~a two-day window, killing 25–27 civilians in Kyiv.
May 13, 2026 saw ~892 total UAVs in a single day — the largest single-night drone figure of 2026.
Other May 2026 nights over 600 drones: May 1 (~619), May 14 (~675), May 24 (~600).
June 2026 total dropped ~30% to ~5,749 Shahed-type UAVs; nights over 600 were June 2 (~656) and June 15 (~611).
Early July 2026 attacks were deadly but BELOW 600 drones: July 1–2 involved 496 drones (570 total assets), and July 4–6 involved 351 drones + 68 missiles.
The July 3–4, 2026 attack killed ~30–31 in Kyiv (deadliest on the capital in 2026) but was defined by lethal targeting/ballistic missiles rather than a record drone count.
Roughly 6–7 identifiable nights in 2026 (through early July) exceeded 600 total drones, all in May and June.
The frequently cited 741/728-drone record is a July 9, 2025 event and must not be conflated with 2026.
Some large June 2026 figures (660 drones, 1,400+) refer to Ukrainian drone attacks on Russia, not Russian attacks on Ukraine.
